text: Šł§√Ę-Mń©m .
By the clear Book!
Indeed, We sent it down on a blessed night, <sup foot_note=1019>1019</sup> for We always warn ňĻagainst evilňļ.

foot_notes.1019: The revelation of the Quran started on the 27 th night of RamaŠłć√Ęn‚ÄĒthe 9 th month of the Islamic calendar ( see 97:1 and 2:185)

text: On that night every matter of wisdom is ordained
by a command from Us, for We have always sent ňĻmessengersňļ
as a mercy from your Lord. He ňĻaloneňļ is truly the All-Hearing, All-Knowing‚ÄĒ
the Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between, if only you had sure faith.
There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. He ňĻaloneňļ gives life and causes death. ňĻHe isňļ your Lord, and the Lord of your forefathers.
In fact, they are in doubt, amusing themselves.
Wait then ňĻO Prophetňļ for the day ňĻwhenňļ the sky will be veiled in haze, <sup foot_note=1021>1021</sup> clearly visible,

foot_notes.1021: This refers to the drought that affected the Meccan pagans so badly that they came to the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) , begging him to pray to Allah to remove the affliction from them.

text: overwhelming the people. ňĻThey will cry,ňļ ‚ÄúThis is a painful torment.
Our Lord! Remove ňĻthisňļ torment from us, ňĻandňļ we will certainly believe.‚ÄĚ
How can they be reminded when a messenger has already come to them, making things clear,
then they turned away from him, saying, ‚ÄúA madman, taught by others!‚ÄĚ?
Indeed, We will remove ňĻthatňļ torment for a while, and you ňĻMeccansňļ will return ňĻto disbeliefňļ.
ňĻThenňļ on the Day We will deal ňĻyouňļ the fiercest blow, We will surely inflict punishment.
Indeed, before them We tested Pharaoh‚Äôs people: a noble messenger came to them,
ňĻproclaiming,ňļ ‚ÄúHand over the servants of Allah to me. <sup foot_note=1022>1022</sup> I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you.
And do not be arrogant with Allah. I have certainly come to you with a compelling proof.
And indeed, I seek refuge with my Lord and your Lord so you do not stone me ňĻto deathňļ.
ňĻButňļ if you do not believe me, then let me be.‚ÄĚ
Ultimately, he cried out to his Lord, ‚ÄúThese are a wicked people!‚ÄĚ
ňĻAllah responded,ňļ ‚ÄúLeave with My servants at night, for you will surely be pursued.
And leave the sea parted, for they are certainly an army bound to drown.‚ÄĚ
ňĻImagineňļ how many gardens and springs the tyrants <sup foot_note=1023>1023</sup> left behind,
as well as ňĻvariousňļ crops and splendid residences,
and luxuries which they fully enjoyed.
So it was. And We awarded it ňĻallňļ to another people.
Neither heaven nor earth wept over them, nor was their fate delayed.
And We certainly delivered the Children of Israel from the humiliating torment
of Pharaoh. He was truly a tyrant, a transgressor.
And indeed, We chose the Israelites knowingly above the others.
And We showed them signs in which there was a clear test.
Indeed, these ňĻMeccansňļ say,
‚ÄúThere is nothing beyond our first death, and we will never be resurrected.
Bring ňĻbackňļ our forefathers, if what you say is true.‚ÄĚ
Are they superior to the people of Tubba‚Äô <sup foot_note=1024>1024</sup> and those before them? We destroyed them ňĻallňļ, ňĻforňļ they were truly wicked.

foot_notes.1024: Tubba‚Äô Al-Šł§imiari was an ancient righteous Yemeni king whose people persisted in disbelief and were destroyed, although they were superior to Meccans in strength and manpower.

text: We did not create the heavens and the earth and everything in between for sport.
We only created them for a purpose, but most of these ňĻpagansňļ do not know.
Surely the Day of ňĻFinalňļ Decision is the time appointed for all‚ÄĒ
the Day no kith or kin will be of benefit to another whatsoever, nor will they be helped,
except those shown mercy by Allah. He is truly the Almighty, Most Merciful.
Surely ňĻthe fruit ofňļ the tree of Zaqq√Ľm <sup foot_note=1025>1025</sup>

foot_notes.1025: Zaqq√Ľm is a tree that grows in the depths of Hell as mentioned in 37:62-65. The pagans of Mecca used to make fun of the Prophet and say, ‚ÄúHow can a tree grow in Hell?‚ÄĚ

text: will be the food of the evildoer.
Like molten metal, it will boil in the bellies
like the boiling of hot water.
ňĻIt will be said,ňļ ‚ÄúSeize them and drag them into the depths of the Hellfire.
Then pour over their heads the torment of boiling water.‚ÄĚ
ňĻThe wicked will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúTaste this. You mighty, noble one! <sup foot_note=1026>1026</sup>

foot_notes.1026: Abu Jahl, a Meccan pagan leader, met the warning of punishment in the Hereafter by exclaiming, ‚ÄúHow can I be punished when I am the mighty, noble one!‚ÄĚ

text: This is truly what you ňĻallňļ used to doubt.‚ÄĚ
Indeed, the righteous will be in a secure place,
amid Gardens and springs,
dressed in fine silk and rich brocade, facing one another.
So it will be. And We will pair them to maidens with gorgeous eyes.
There they will call for every fruit in serenity.
There they will never taste death, beyond the first death. And He will protect them from the punishment of the Hellfire‚ÄĒ
as ňĻan act ofňļ grace from your Lord. That is ňĻtrulyňļ the ultimate triumph.
Indeed, We have made this ňĻQuranňļ easy in your own language ňĻO Prophetňļ so perhaps they will be mindful.
Wait then! They too are certainly waiting.
